priorities: Country economy pages have the highest per-page SEO value — each targets a unique head term
By-country landing pages target high-volume queries like 'X by country 2026'
Blog posts target long-tail question queries for featured snippets
Glossary terms build topical authority and internal link density
Comparison editorials add unique editorial value to comparison pages
E-E-A-T pages (methodology, about, data sources) build trust signals
Schema markup improvements help with rich results
Internal linking passes distribute PageRank to new pages
learnings: Each country economy page needs: unique 500+ word editorial, 8 live data cards, FAQ schema, comparison links, cross-links
By-country pages need: Dataset schema, FAQ schema, editorial, color-coded data table, breadcrumbs
Blog posts are programmatic — just add to BLOG_POSTS array with indicatorId, they auto-generate
Comparison editorials go in COMPARISON_EDITORIAL Record in compare/[slug]/page.tsx
Glossary terms go in GLOSSARY_TERMS array in lib/glossary.ts
All new pages must be added to sitemap in lib/sitemap.ts
Build must pass (npx next build) before committing
Push to master auto-deploys via Coolify webhook
Duplicate comparison keys cause build failures — always check for existing keys before adding
Run 1 completed: added Malaysia, Colombia, Philippines, Bangladesh, Pakistan economy pages
Country economy pages are the highest-value SEO asset per page — each targets '[country] economy' head term
Compact page format works well — single-file with inline data cards, editorial, cross-links
Run 2 completed: added Chile, Czech Republic, Romania, UAE, Peru economy pages (40 total country pages)
Run 3 completed: added New Zealand, Portugal, Greece, Hungary, Denmark (45 total country pages)
Run 4 completed: added Finland, Austria, Belgium, Kenya, Ethiopia (50 total country pages)
Run 5 completed: added Morocco, Algeria, Ukraine, Kazakhstan, Qatar (55 total country pages)
All major pending country pages done — shift to by-country landing pages and content improvements next
